title: practical english usage - Part 108
content:
(More natural than A number of people has tried...) A number of people have tried to find the treasure, but they have all failed.

and pronouns; plural verbs are normally used in this case.

Many singular quantifying expressions (►    172) can be used with plural nouns 2 A number         of   people        have      ...

grammar •  130   mixed singular and plural: other structures verb agree with a complement.

However, if the subject is a long way from the verb, people sometimes make the A serious problem in our garden is wasps.

In English a verb normally agrees with the subject of a sentence, not with a 2 A serious        problem         is  wasps.

We've got one of those Japanese cars that never break/breaks down.

answer emails.

One of the things that really make/makes me angry is people who don't structures. More examples: those books that). However, singular verbs are also very common in these Strictly speaking, a plural verb is correct (to agree with the few women who or This is one of those books that are/is read by everybody.

She's one of the few women who have/has climbed Everest.

used in relative clauses beginning who, which or that.

After expressions like one of the... (►  544), singular and plural verbs are both 1  one     of   the    few     women        who     have      climbed         Everest singular and plural elements.

expressions, one singular and the other plural. And some noun phrases mix In some complex structures, the same verb seems to belong with two different 130 mixed     singular      and   plural:    other     structures Consolidated Fruitgrowers has just taken over Universal Foodstores.

Plural names of organisations may also have singular verbs and pronouns.

The United States is anxious to improve its image in Latin America.

Plural names of countries usually have singular verbs and pronouns.

6 countries       and   organisations:         The     United        States       is  . . .

Your toast and honey is on the table.

This gin and tonic isn’ t very strong is it?

think of them as a single idea.

(►    130.4). This happens when the two nouns are used together so often that we Some expressions joined by and have singular determiners, verbs and pronouns 5 and Section11 Nouns     and   Noun    Phrases;    Agreement grammar •  130   mixed singular and plural: other structures Neither she nor her husband have arrived. (informal) Neither she nor her husband has arrived. (formal) singular in a formal style, but can be plural in an informal style.

When  two singular subjects are joined by neither. .. nor, the verb is normally There's no room - either a table or two chairs have got to be moved.

There's no room - either two chairs or a table has got to be moved.

subject is singular, and plural if it is plural. Compare: When two subjects are joined by or, the verb is usually singular if the second new budget.

The Managing Director, together with his heads of department, is preparing a financial policy.

The Prime Minister, as well as several Cabinet Ministers, believes in a tough the verb is usually singular if the first subject is singular.

When  two subjects are joined by as well as, together with or a similar expression, Bacon and eggs is a popular British breakfast.

singular verbs (►   129.5).

But note that some phrases with and are treated like single ideas, and used with Alice and Joseph are going to be late.

When  two singular subjects are joined by and, the verb is normally plural.

4 co-ordinated         subjects:     A and     B, A or   B, A as   well     as   B, etc For singular or plural after kind(s), sort(s), type(s), etc, ►    592.

(More correct: A good knowledge... is . ..) A good knowledge of three languages are necessary for this job.
